utility_evaluation.REASONING: Required format has 5 items: health_coverage, property_coverage, travel_coverage, additional_services, total_budget. The final plan includes all five top-level items. Now check if each is fully specified with all details as required: (1) health_coverage: specifies physical and mental health insurance and costs, plus a total health cost; meets requirement. (2) property_coverage: specifies home/contents insurance with costs, plus a total property cost; meets requirement (though only contents is specified, the format asks for home/contents, but at least contents with costs is present; still counts as property_coverage item being filled with costs). (3) travel_coverage: includes travel insurance with costs and total; meets requirement. (4) additional_services: lists enhancement services with costs (noted as included at no extra cost); meets requirement. (5) total_budget: provides monthly and annual cost breakdown; meets requirement. Therefore, all 5/5 items are included and fully specified according to the format demands.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: The final coverage plan includes three specific policy names that appear in the ground-truth ratings: LifeFirst Standard Plan (for physical health), LifeFirst Standard Plan mental health (referred to as LifeFirst Standard Mental Health at €65/month), InsurePlus Standard Coverage (contents insurance), and LifeFirst Standard Plan for travel (referred to as LifeFirst Standard Travel at €50/month). I matched each to the exact corresponding entries in the ground-truth and extracted their ratings.
